author: PC Gamer
text:
'Generational talent just gone': Developers react in shock at the brutal scale of Bungie layoffs, which has reportedly seen the Destiny 2 team almost completely wiped out
Many of those being let go are showing incredible dignity in the face of a gigantic squandering of talent.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Deltarune Chapter 5 launches to nearly 300,000 concurrent players on Steam, Chapter 6 is coming in 2027
People sure seem to like Deltarune.

author: PC Gamer
text:
1 year after launch, spaceship shooter Wildgate will end development after its next update
Thankfully, it's not shutting down, and developer Moonshot Games says no layoffs are planned.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've played countless Doom engine games, so believe me when I say this one that combines the FPS with JRPG combat might be the coolest one in 30 years
Free Doom total conversion End of Starlight is part bullet hell, part surprise RPG, all enigma.

author: PC Gamer
text:
PC Gamer's 2025 GOTY is yours for a ludicrous $24 during the Steam Summer Sale
Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is 60% off, and Warhorse is celebrating with a free quest.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I dare you to refuse these unprecedented Prime Day deals on miniature shrubbery
Don't look away. I know what you are.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The best farm sim since Stardew Valley is on sale right before its 1.0 launch
Fields of Mistria is leaving Early Access in August, so now is a perfect time to grab it at a discount.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's not layoffs, but you can see them from here: Numerous Compulsion Games employees are looking for new jobs
The sudden influx of "open to work" posts suggests that fears of major Xbox layoffs in the near future are well founded.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's wild of Rockstar to ask us for $80, minimum, without showing a GTA 6 gameplay trailer
I really didn't think they'd open pre-orders without a new trailer, but here we are.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Steam Summer Sale goes live with 'deep-a** discounts' across thousands of games
Don't blame me, that's what they said.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve says Steam Machine won't have 'as long of a timeframe' as Steam Deck, and new models are 'just a matter of when it makes sense'
The Steam Machine is passé. I'm all about the Steam Machine 2.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We should all be happy that GTA 6 isn't launching with multiplayer
A GTAO sequel is almost certainly coming, but the singleplayer campaign should be the priority.

author: PC Gamer
text:
GTA 6 pre-order editions explained: What do you get with each version?
Decide whether to opt for standard, ultimate, or wait a while.

author: PC Gamer
text:
With Destiny 2 over, Bungie lays off 'most of the Destiny team and some Marathon team members'
The number of people put out of work has not been disclosed, but it doesn't sound good.

author: PC Gamer
text:
When the heck is GTA 6 going to release on PC?
We're expecting to wait about a year, and for Rockstar to keep playing coy about the PC version.

author: PC Gamer
text:
GTA 6 pre-orders are now live, but will you buy on console or hold out for the (obviously superior) PC version?
Or will you double dip... as Rockstar wants?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find bamboo in Forza Horizon 6
Break these green shoots for the Bamboo Bash challenge.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The RAM crisis comes for Apple: Mac and iPad prices jacked up by hundreds as company says 'We have never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly'
Think different (prices).

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve wasn't sure it was gonna have any Steam Machines to sell at the start of 2026: 'Things looked really dire'
Another thing to thank the RAM crisis for.

author: PC Gamer
text:
My favourite new roguelike is all about evolution and crabs, and it's celebrating 500k sales with a big update
Everything is Crab has evolved.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Chuck Tingle's latest butt-centric work of erotic fiction is about Warhammer 40,000 of all things
Turns out, the Tingle is a Warhammer dork of the most relatable kind.

author: PC Gamer
text:
18-year-old strategy game King's Bounty: The Legend just got a quality-of-life update, as well as achievements and a Steam Workshop for mods
Time to go back and marry another oddball princess.

author: PC Gamer
text:
GTA 6's preorders mark the start of a long wait for it to come to PC, despite the fact we'll likely make up '50% of the sales'
What are we, chopped liver?

author: PC Gamer
text:
You can finally turn Riot's Vanguard anti-cheat off when you're not playing a game
Most of you, anyway.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Now we know how much GTA 6 costs, but can you remember what these other games cost at launch? Take our price tag quiz!
There's always a price.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Tim Sweeney on the future of games, AI, and whether Valve will ever join forces with Epic: 'It's now clear that nobody's going to end up with an absolute monopoly'
The Epic Games CEO discusses his vision for "Team Open,"  his objections to Steam's AI disclosure requirement, and the huge problems facing AAA game development.

author: PC Gamer
text:
John Carmack apologizes after Sandy Petersen says 'Quake ruined id Software,' and for once John Romero doesn't tell Sandy he's wrong
Three of the principals from the early days of id Software shared some faintly maudlin but ultimately uplifting reminiscences from those long-ago days.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Don't blame yourselves' for Destiny 2's end, community lead tells players: 'No words can accurately depict how lucky we are to have shared these worlds with you'
As Destiny 2 reaches the end, dmg04 has some reassuring words for mourning fans.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Elden Ring 'profit leakage' has Kadokawa investors looking for a new boss
If you've ever wondered why company executives prioritize shareholders over customers, well, here it is.
